{{Quote|Captain Rooks… contact the Mandalore… I will give him the terms of his surrender.|Ulic Qel-Droma to Rooks|<ref name="thesithwar" />}}
'''Rooks''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] and a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the service of the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] during the era of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]].<ref name="thesithwar" /> Rooks [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] a Krath patrol [[Starship/Legends|ship]],<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and in [[3996 BBY]], he served on a [[Krath command yacht]] under two of the Krath's leaders; fallen [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-turned-[[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] and his lover [[Aleema Keto]]. While Rooks was present on the command [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]], the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Tetan]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] came under attack from a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] strike force, commanded by their leader [[Mandalore the Indomitable]]. After the Mandalorians caused some damage to the Tetans, Qel-Droma ordered Rooks to open up a line of communication with the Mandalore. Rooks obeyed the [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith's]] order, and established a video feed to the Mandalore's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]. The Mandalore took the opportunity to challenge Qel-Droma to single combat, an offer the Jedi accepted.<ref name="thesithwar" />

==Behind the scenes==
Rooks first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1995]]. In Rooks' only appearance in the issue, he was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]<ref name="thesithwar" /> Rooks was also given mentions in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''<ref name="swe">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="cswe" />
